title: UV Crosslinking of Adherent Cells for eCLIP
author: Eric Van Nostrand
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.z6zf9f6
materials: Gently resuspend until the sample is homogeneous

steps.title: Place the tissue culture plate on...
steps.instruction: Place the tissue culture plate on leveled ice or a cooling block pre-chilled to 4°C

steps.title: Place the above (plate plus ice...
steps.instruction: Place the above (plate plus ice or cooling block) into the UV cross-linker. Note: Ensure the plate is leveled. Remove tissue culture plate lid for cross-linking.

steps.title: Transfer the cells to a 50 mL conical tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the cells to a 50 mL conical tube

steps.title: Wash the plate gently with 1x...
steps.instruction: Wash the plate gently with 1x DPBS at room temperature (15 mL for a 15 cm plate).

steps.title: Add enough 1x DPBS to just...
steps.instruction: Add enough 1x DPBS to just cover the plate (5 mL for a 15 cm plate)

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: If all plates are at equal cell density, one plate can be sacrificed for counting – this plate would be dissociated (with trypsin, accutase, or equivalent) and cell number (per plate) counted at this stage. This is recommended for cell types that require chemical dissociation enzymes to dissociate and be properly counted, as this is not recommended post-crosslinking.

steps.title: Wash plate once with 10mL of...
steps.instruction: Wash plate once with 10mL of 1x DPBS and add to the same 50 mL tube.

steps.title: While keeping the cells on ice,...
steps.instruction: While keeping the cells on ice, use a cell scraper (Corning, CLS3010-10EA) to scrape the plate.

steps.title: Cross-link at 254-nm UV with an...
steps.instruction: Cross-link at 254-nm UV with an energy setting of 400 mJoules/cm 2 Note: this is a setting of 4000 on many cross-linkers which display values in 0.1 mJoules/cm 2

steps.title: Count cell concentration (either with automated...
steps.instruction: Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer) Note : ensure cells are re-suspended well before counting Note : for cells that do not easily dissociate into single cells, a separate plate of cells can be counted instead (see * above)

steps.title: Resuspend in the desired amount for...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in the desired amount for flash freezing Typically 20×10 6 cells per mL.

steps.title: Centrifuge the 50 ml conical tube...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 50 ml conical tube at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: After frozen (at least thirty seconds),...
steps.instruction: After frozen (at least thirty seconds), remove from the liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and freeze by...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and freeze by submerging the epi-tubes completely in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Spin down at 200g for five minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Spin down at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Transfer desired amount into 1
steps.instruction: Transfer desired amount into 1.5 mL Eppendorf Safe-Lock Tubes (or equivalent) Typically 1mL of 20×10 6 cells per mL.
